items.title: Finish the Shell source-of-truth cleanup
items.summary: Finish the Shell source-of-truth cleanup by closing the duplicate TS inventory and breaking chat.ts into canonical modules instead of mirrored artifacts.

items.title: Move chat and session projections behind Gateway
items.source_family: Shell Authority Extraction Without Legacy Shell Mutation
items.summary: Make chat/session list and message-window loading available as bounded Gateway projections with CLI/headless proof, so the Shell no longer needs full conversation ownership.

items.title: Move interactive command authority out of Shell
items.source_family: Shell Authority Extraction Without Legacy Shell Mutation
items.summary: Route slash commands, model switching, model failover, prompt queue execution, and terminal execution through typed Gateway ingress receipts instead of browser-owned helpers.

items.title: Move message and tool details behind lazy routes
items.source_family: Shell Authority Extraction Without Legacy Shell Mutation
items.summary: Expose message, tool, artifact, trace, and workflow detail fetches by stable refs through bounded audited Gateway routes instead of default Shell payloads.

items.title: Finish the Shell authority purge
items.summary: Finish the Shell authority purge so the Shell becomes projection/input only and stops acting like a shadow runtime.

items.title: Keep active SRS intake items moving
items.summary: Keep active SRS intake items moving before opening more new fronts.

items.title: Remove the remaining Alpine boot/runtime dependency
items.summary: Remove the remaining Alpine boot/runtime dependency once the retirement guard is green.

items.title: Replace Shell conversation cache and search with projections
items.source_family: Shell Authority Extraction Without Legacy Shell Mutation
items.summary: Move conversation cache/search to bounded preview/index routes so the browser keeps only cursors, previews, counts, and refs rather than cloned full messages or raw tool payloads.

items.title: Move report/eval issue flow behind Gateway ingress
items.source_family: Shell Authority Extraction Without Legacy Shell Mutation
items.summary: Make chat-local report issue send bounded refs/context through Gateway to Orchestration eval without Shell-owned policy, GitHub submission, or raw context upload.

items.title: Make health and connectivity purely Gateway projected
items.source_family: Shell Authority Extraction Without Legacy Shell Mutation
items.summary: Make runtime health, connectivity, release status, and degraded-state labels come from bounded Gateway status projections so Shell does not infer readiness or failure truth.

items.title: Move agent and session lifecycle to receipts
items.source_family: Shell Authority Extraction Without Legacy Shell Mutation
items.summary: Ensure create, select, archive, reset, retry, and new-agent initialization flows work through Gateway/Core/Orchestration receipts with headless proof.

items.title: Project workflow stage and thought status from Orchestration
items.source_family: Shell Authority Extraction Without Legacy Shell Mutation
items.summary: Make Orchestration emit workflow_stage_label and workflow_thought_preview projections so thinking bubbles render owner-provided status instead of Shell-authored fallback text.

items.title: Build the workflow utility spine
items.source_family: Workflow Utility Spine After Shell Purge
items.summary: Build the workflow utility spine so the system is useful for real work after Shell de-authority.

items.title: Implement end-to-end unified trace_id propagation
items.summary: Implement end-to-end unified trace_id propagation from initial request through Orchestration, workflows, tools, Kernel receipts, Sentinel, and final response.

items.title: Hold important architecture and tooling deltas behind current closure work
items.summary: Important architecture and tooling deltas should follow the Shell, workflow, and trace closure work rather than compete with it immediately.

items.title: Queue the next SRS stream after the red section
items.summary: Queue the next SRS stream after the red intake set so the active SRS flow stays coherent instead of fragmenting into too many parallel themes.

items.title: Build Shell-next only after authority extraction proofs
items.source_family: Shell Authority Extraction Without Legacy Shell Mutation
items.summary: Start the clean Shell-next route as a projection/input-only UI after the headless capability matrix and high-risk authority extraction routes are proven.

items.title: Keep remaining Svelte migration subordinate to Shell authority purge
items.source_family: Chat Dashboard Alpine to Svelte Migration Wave
items.summary: Remaining Svelte migration and memory profiling items are worth doing, but they are subordinate to the broader Shell authority purge.

items.title: Keep externally blocked work parked in the archive
items.summary: External blockers from the previous ledger remain parked in the archive until the required evidence packets or human approvals exist.

items.title: Park longer-horizon assimilation and runtime work
items.summary: Assimilation and longer-horizon runtime work can stay parked here until the red and yellow closure work is materially better.

items.title: Keep lower-pressure valid backlog items parked
items.summary: These are still valid backlog items, but they are not the current forcing function.

items.title: Keep Layer 3 and OS-readiness work parked behind current closure work
items.source_family: OS-Readiness Wave - Layer 3 to True OS Migration
items.summary: Layer 3 and OS-readiness work remains important, but it should stay behind the current Shell, workflow, and trace closure push.

items.title: Keep Tauri migration strategically queued
items.source_family: Tauri Desktop App Migration + Memory Fix Wave
items.summary: Tauri migration remains strategically valuable, but it should not outrun the deeper architecture cleanup already underway.

items.title: Audit and graduate Digital DNA as system substrate
items.summary: Yellow flag: Digital DNA has real Kernel metakernel code and tests, but SRS/TODO still mark V6-FOUNDATION-DNA-001/002 queued and it is not proven as the unavoidable base substrate for every instance, mutation, critical action, receipt, and Sentinel integrity check.

items.title: Capture Rust dead-code warning baseline
items.summary: Capture the current cargo dead-code and unused-warning surface by crate, module, warning kind, and likely action so future cleanup can happen in safe rollback-sized batches.

items.title: Remove unused Rust imports in narrow batches
items.summary: Work through unused import warnings in Kernel and runtime crates in small scoped commits, preserving behavior and validating each touched crate with cargo check or cargo test.

items.title: Triage unused Rust helper functions
items.summary: For unused private helpers, decide whether each should be deleted, reconnected to the intended path, moved behind tests, or marked as intentional compatibility surface with a documented reason.

items.title: Classify combined Rust split artifacts
items.summary: Classify 000-combined.rs and .combined_parts warning-heavy files as generated split debt, live source, or deletion candidates before attempting broad cleanup.

items.title: Add Rust dead-code trend guard
items.summary: Add or extend a validation report that tracks Rust unused/dead-code warning counts by crate so the surface trends down instead of silently growing.

items.title: Inventory combined Rust artifacts
items.summary: Generate a manifest of every tracked 000-combined.rs and .combined_parts artifact with path, crate, line count, include/module parent, warning count, and likely owner before deleting or renaming anything.

items.title: Map combined artifact references
items.summary: For each combined artifact, map whether it is included by live Rust modules, tests only, generated tooling, docs only, or unreferenced so cleanup can be behavior-preserving.

items.title: Delete dead combined artifacts in safe batches
items.summary: Delete only artifacts classified as dead_artifact or unreferenced, in narrow rollback-sized commits with cargo check or targeted tests for the owning crate.

items.title: Decompose live combined artifact debt
items.summary: For live split_debt artifacts, replace generic 000-combined.rs and .combined_parts names with logical module names over time, prioritizing non-shell, non-orchestration Kernel/ops areas with targeted validation.

items.title: Guard against new combined artifacts
items.summary: Add a validation guard or policy row that blocks new 000-combined.rs and .combined_parts artifacts unless they declare an explicit generated-source or compatibility exception with owner and expiry.

items.title: Make CodeQL remediation the first canonical real-work workflow
items.summary: Define the reusable alert-to-patch loop: ingest CodeQL alert, locate file and rule, patch narrowly, run targeted validation, commit with a conventional prefix, and record the closure pattern.

items.title: Make Sentinel findings issue-ready before promotion
items.summary: Require promoted Sentinel findings to include evidence, recurrence or freshness, owner guess, root-cause hypothesis, proposed fix class, targeted validation suggestion, and TODO or issue wording.

items.title: Define the minimum reliable real-work paths
items.summary: Keep install and repair, gateway lifecycle, request-to-response execution, alert remediation, Sentinel finding promotion, TODO lifecycle, and commit/release hygiene as the reliability floor.

items.title: Make the sacred workflow explicit and reusable
items.summary: Document and wire the common loop from request, issue, alert, or Sentinel finding through evidence inspection, ownership, patch, targeted validation, explanation, and TODO or issue update.

items.title: Add a simplification filter to TODO intake
items.summary: Reject or park active items that do not improve real work, reliability, or simplification; prefer deletion and compression over new subsystems unless the sacred workflow benefits.

items.title: Require new subsystems to prove real-work value first
items.summary: Before adding a new subsystem, require a concrete user-facing or agent-facing workflow it improves, the reliability risk it reduces, and the simpler alternative considered.
